title: Sorolepidium duthiei
taxonomy: Sorolepidium duthiei is classified as a genus and species of a tetrapodomorph, which is a group of ancient, four-limbed vertebrates. It belongs to the family Plourdosteidae, a group of extinct fish-like tetrapods. This genus and species is considered to be a type of early tetrapod, with a mix of fish-like and terrestrial characteristics. Sorolepidium duthiei is known from fossil remains found in India, specifically from the Late Carboniferous to Early Permian period. The classification of Sorolepidium duthiei is based on its unique combination of morphological features, which have helped scientists to understand the evolution of early tetrapods.
